title: psychotria wightiana
intro: Psychotria wightiana, also known as Wight's psychotria or Wight's wild coffee, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In Ayurvedic preparations, the leaves of the plant are commonly used as a tea or are added to herbal formulations, such as decoctions and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
